resources.string: Pressing a panel button calls the contact assigned to it
The phone language is used by default. You can choose a different language here.
Complete these steps so the button works while the phone is locked.
Open settings and turn it off
Allow Nearby devices and Phone access to detect presses and make a call.
Allow notifications so Android can keep monitoring active in the background and play a confirmation.
In the window that opens, choose “Allow” to prevent monitoring from pausing while the screen is locked.
On the screen that opens, turn off “Manage app if unused,” then return to the app.
Enter at least one phone number. The change is saved automatically.
Discovery is active. Press any panel button once and keep the panel near the phone.
You can assign a different number to each button. An empty field disables that button. Include the country code when possible.
Optional — leave empty to disable the button
All four buttons call this number
The button panel has not been found yet
Discovery starts automatically. Press any panel button once and keep it near the phone.
The button panel is connected and ready.
The press type applies to every button with an assigned phone number.
No event has been received from the button panel yet
Press any panel button once. Keep the panel near the phone.
Enter at least one phone number
Wait until the button panel is found
Waiting for a press to call the assigned contact
Plays an alarm sound and two vibrations when a press starts a call
Nearby devices and Phone permissions are required
Turn on Bluetooth. Discovery will continue automatically.
Bluetooth LE is unavailable on this phone
Android could not register the Bluetooth scan
Android reported a Bluetooth scan error
This phone does not support the required Bluetooth scan
No Bluetooth scanning resources are available on this phone
Android temporarily limited Bluetooth scanning; try again soon
Could not search for the button panel: %1$s
Button panel found — monitoring can now be started
Monitoring is active in the background
Bluetooth monitoring error %1$d; open Sara Button
Monitoring will retry after Bluetooth error %1$d
Monitoring is waiting for Bluetooth to be turned on
Monitoring will retry because Bluetooth is unavailable
Monitoring will retry after a Bluetooth interruption
The button panel is encrypted; open Sara Button for reset instructions

The phone detects the panel, but its BTHome broadcasts are encrypted. To reset it: remove and reinsert the battery, then within 3 minutes hold any button for 30 seconds until the LED turns red. This erases existing Shelly or Zigbee settings. Discovery will then restart automatically.

The phone detects the Tough button, but its BTHome broadcasts are encrypted. To reset it, hold the button for 30 seconds until the factory-reset melody plays, then release it. Continuing to hold for another 10 seconds cancels the reset. Discovery will then restart automatically.
Cannot call: phone number is missing
Cannot call: Phone permission is missing
Monitoring is active — a call was just started
Cannot call: Android phone service is unavailable
A call is already active — the additional press was canceled
Monitoring is active — the call is being placed
Android could not place the call
Android could not place the call: %1$s
On Android 10–11, choose “Allow all the time” Location access so Bluetooth monitoring works while the app is not visible. The app never derives or stores location.
Background monitoring requires “Allow all the time” Location access on Android 10–11
